sEn: Aletheia, a math research agent, achieves milestones from Olympiad to PhD-level problems and autonomous research papers.
sZh: Aletheia数学研究智能体从奥赛到博士级问题取得突破，并自主生成研究论文。
absEn: Recent advances in foundational models have yielded reasoning systems capable of achieving a gold-medal standard at the International Mathematical Olympiad. The transition from competition-level problem-solving to professional research, however, requires navigating vast literature and constructing long-horizon proofs. In this work, we introduce Aletheia, a math research agent that iteratively generates, verifies, and revises solutions end-to-end in natural language. Specifically, Aletheia is powered by an advanced version of Gemini Deep Think for challenging reasoning problems, a novel inference-time scaling law that extends beyond Olympiad-level problems, and intensive tool use to navigate the complexities of mathematical research. We demonstrate the capability of Aletheia from Olympiad problems to PhD-level exercises and most notably, through several distinct milestones in AI-assisted mathematics research: (a) a research paper (Feng26) generated by AI without any human intervention in calculating certain structure constants in arithmetic geometry called eigenweights; (b) a research paper (LeeSeo26) demonstrating human-AI collaboration in proving bounds on systems of interacting particles called independent sets; and (c) an extensive semi-autonomous evaluation (Feng et al., 2026a) of 700 open problems on Bloom's Erdos Conjectures database, including autonomous solutions to four open questions. In order to help the public better understand the developments pertaining to AI and mathematics, we suggest quantifying standard levels of autonomy and novelty of AI-assisted results, as well as propose a novel concept of human-AI interaction cards for transparency. We conclude with reflections on human-AI collaboration in mathematics and share all prompts as well as model outputs at https://github.com/google-deepmind/superhuman/tree/main/aletheia.
absZh: 基础模型的最新进展催生了能在国际数学奥林匹克竞赛中达到金牌水平的推理系统。然而，从竞赛级问题解决到专业研究，需要浏览大量文献并构建长程证明。本文介绍了Aletheia，一个数学研究智能体，它以自然语言端到端地迭代生成、验证和修正解决方案。具体来说，Aletheia由高级版Gemini Deep Think驱动，用于解决具有挑战性的推理问题，这是一种超越奥赛级问题的新型推理时缩放定律，并密集使用工具来应对数学研究的复杂性。我们展示了Aletheia从奥赛问题到博士级练习的能力，最值得注意的是，在AI辅助数学研究中取得了几个里程碑：(a) 一篇由AI完全自主生成的研究论文（Feng26），计算了算术几何中称为特征权的某些结构常数；(b) 一篇展示人机协作的研究论文（LeeSeo26），证明了相互作用粒子系统（独立集）的界；(c) 对Bloom的Erdős猜想数据库中700个开放问题进行了广泛的半自主评估（Feng et al., 2026a），包括自主解决了四个开放问题。为了帮助公众更好地理解AI与数学相关的发展，我们建议量化AI辅助结果的自主性和新颖性标准，并提出一种新的人机交互卡概念以提高透明度。最后，我们反思了数学中的人机协作，并在https://github.com/google-deepmind/superhuman/tree/main/aletheia分享了所有提示和模型输出。

contrib.en: Introduces Aletheia, a math research agent that iteratively generates, verifies, and revises solutions end-to-end in natural language.
contrib.zh: 提出Aletheia，一个数学研究智能体，能够以自然语言端到端地迭代生成、验证和修正解决方案。

contrib.en: Demonstrates a novel inference-time scaling law extending from Olympiad-level to PhD-level problems.
contrib.zh: 展示了一种新的推理时缩放定律，从奥赛级别问题扩展到博士级别问题。

contrib.en: Achieves autonomous generation of a research paper (Feng26) calculating eigenweights in arithmetic geometry without human intervention.
contrib.zh: 实现了自主生成研究论文（Feng26），无需人工干预即可计算算术几何中的特征权重。

contrib.en: Presents human-AI collaboration in proving bounds on independent sets (LeeSeo26), with AI providing high-level strategy.
contrib.zh: 展示了人机协作证明独立集边界（LeeSeo26），AI提供了高层策略。

contrib.en: Conducts semi-autonomous evaluation on 700 open Erdős problems, autonomously solving four open questions.
contrib.zh: 对700个开放的Erdős问题进行了半自主评估，自主解决了四个开放问题。

contrib.en: Proposes standard levels of autonomy and novelty for AI-assisted mathematics, and human-AI interaction cards for transparency.
contrib.zh: 提出了AI辅助数学的自主性和新颖性标准等级，以及用于透明化的人机交互卡。

limits.en: Success cases are rare; most research-level problems saw no autonomous progress.
limits.zh: 成功案例罕见；大多数研究级问题未取得自主进展。

limits.en: Model is highly prone to errors and hallucinations, especially in ambiguous problem interpretations.
limits.zh: 模型极易出错和产生幻觉，尤其是在问题解释存在歧义时。

limits.en: Autonomous results are relatively brief and elementary compared to typical human research papers.
limits.zh: 与典型的人类研究论文相比，自主结果相对简短且基础。

limits.en: Model exhibits specification gaming, misinterpreting questions to make them easier to answer.
limits.zh: 模型表现出规范博弈，曲解问题以使其更容易回答。

limits.en: Evaluation of mathematical significance requires expert mathematicians, limiting scalability.
limits.zh: 数学意义的评估需要专家数学家，限制了可扩展性。

items.en: Recent advances in foundational models have yielded reasoning systems capable of achieving a gold-medal standard at the International Mathematical Olympiad. The transition from competition-level problem solving to professional research, however, requires navigating vast literature and constructing long-horizon proofs. In this work, we introduce Aletheia, a math research agent that iteratively generates, verifies, and revises solutions end-to-end in natural language. Specifically, Aletheia is powered by by three main sources: (i) an advanced version of Gemini Deep Think for challenging reasoning problems, (ii) a novel inference-time scaling law that extends beyond Olympiad-level problems, and (iii) intensive tool use to navigate the complexities of mathematical research. We demonstrate the capability of Aletheia from Olympiad problems to PhD-level exercises and most notably, through several distinct milestones in AI-assisted mathematics research: (a) a research paper [Feng2026] generated by AI without any human intervention in calculating certain structure constants in arithmetic geometry called eigenweights; (b) a research paper [LeeSeo2026] demonstrating human-AI collaboration in proving bounds on systems of interacting particles called independent sets; and (c) an extensive semi-autonomous evaluation erdos of 700 open problems on Bloom’s Erdős Conjectures database, including autonomous solutions to four open questions. In order to help the public better understand the developments pertaining to AI and mathematics, we suggest quantifying standard levels of autonomy and novelty of AI-assisted results, as well as propose a novel concept of human-AI interaction cards for transparency. We conclude with reflections on human-AI collaboration in mathematics and share all prompts as well as model outputs at https://github.com/google-deepmind/superhuman/tree/main/aletheia.
items.zh: 基础模型的最新进展已产生能够在国际数学奥林匹克竞赛中达到金牌标准的推理系统。然而，从竞赛级问题解决到专业研究的过渡，需要浏览大量文献并构建长程证明。在这项工作中，我们介绍了Aletheia，一个数学研究智能体，能够以自然语言端到端地迭代生成、验证和修正解决方案。具体来说，Aletheia由三个主要来源驱动：(i) 用于挑战性推理问题的高级版Gemini Deep Think，(ii) 一种超越奥林匹克级问题的新型推理时缩放定律，以及(iii) 密集的工具使用以应对数学研究的复杂性。我们展示了Aletheia从奥林匹克问题到博士级练习的能力，最值得注意的是，通过人工智能辅助数学研究的几个不同里程碑：(a) 一篇研究论文[Feng2026]，由AI在无需任何人工干预的情况下生成，计算了算术几何中称为特征权重的某些结构常数；(b) 一篇研究论文[LeeSeo2026]，展示了人机协作证明称为独立集的相互作用粒子系统的界限；(c) 对Bloom的Erdős猜想数据库中700个开放问题进行了广泛的半自主评估，包括对四个开放问题的自主解决方案。为了帮助公众更好地理解AI与数学相关的发展，我们建议量化AI辅助结果的自主性和新颖性标准水平，并提出一种新颖的人机交互卡概念以提高透明度。最后，我们反思了数学中的人机协作，并在https://github.com/google-deepmind/superhuman/tree/main/aletheia分享了所有提示和模型输出。

items.en: Recent years have witnessed rapid progress in the reasoning capabilities of natural language-based AI in competition mathematics luong2025robustmathematicalreasoning. Notably, AI models have already achieved gold-medal performance in the 2025 International Mathematical Olympiad (IMO) imo-gold, widely regarded as the world’s premier mathematics competition. This milestone motivates a fundamental question regarding AI-driven scientific discovery: can AI autonomously discover and prove new mathematical theorems? Transitioning from contest to research mathematics presents significant challenges. Unlike self-contained competition problems, research questions require synthesizing advanced techniques from an extensive body of literature. This poses a major hurdle for foundation or large language models, which often hallucinate and exhibit only a superficial understanding of specialized topics—limitations likely stemming from a scarcity of relevant training data.
items.zh: 近年来，基于自然语言的AI在竞赛数学中的推理能力取得了快速进展（luong2025robustmathematicalreasoning）。值得注意的是，AI模型已在2025年国际数学奥林匹克竞赛（IMO）中达到金牌水平，该竞赛被广泛认为是世界顶级数学竞赛。这一里程碑引发了一个关于AI驱动科学发现的基本问题：AI能否自主发现并证明新的数学定理？从竞赛到研究数学的过渡带来了重大挑战。与自包含的竞赛问题不同，研究问题需要综合大量文献中的先进技术。这对基础模型或大语言模型构成了重大障碍，这些模型常常产生幻觉，且仅对专业主题有肤浅理解——这些限制可能源于相关训练数据的稀缺。

items.en: To explore this challenge, we developed Aletheia, a math research agent that can iteratively generate, verify, and revise solutions end-to-end in natural language. Aletheia is powered by three main sources: (i) an advanced version of Gemini Deep Think deepmind:gemini2p5deepthink for tackling extremely hard reasoning problems, (ii) a novel inference-time scaling law that extends from Olympiad-level problems to PhD-level exercises, and (iii) intensive tool use such as Google Search and web browsing to navigate the complexities of mathematical research. On Olympiad-level problems, Aletheia, without internet access, has achieved a new state-of-the-art performance of 95.1%95.1\% accuracy on the advanced version of IMO-ProofBench luong2025robustmathematicalreasoning, according to human experts111This significantly outperforms the previous best result of 65.7%65.7\% achieved by Gemini Deep Think (IMO Gold) in July 2025. See full leaderboard in https://imobench.github.io/.. Similarly, on an internal benchmark (FutureMath Basic) of PhD-level exercises, Aletheia also demonstrated state-of-the-art performance. Notably, this paper presents and reflects on the initial wave of mathematical research papers achieved by Aletheia222See our project page https://github.com/google-deepmind/superhuman/tree/main/aletheia for detailed prompts and model outputs. in collaboration with mathematicians:
items.zh: 为了探索这一挑战，我们开发了Aletheia，一个数学研究智能体，能够以自然语言端到端地迭代生成、验证和修正解决方案。Aletheia由三个主要来源驱动：(i) 用于处理极难推理问题的高级版Gemini Deep Think（deepmind:gemini2p5deepthink），(ii) 一种从奥林匹克级问题扩展到博士级练习的新型推理时缩放定律，以及(iii) 密集的工具使用，如Google搜索和网页浏览，以应对数学研究的复杂性。在奥林匹克级问题上，Aletheia（无互联网访问）在IMO-ProofBench的高级版本上达到了95.1%的新最优准确率（根据人类专家评估¹¹¹这显著优于2025年7月Gemini Deep Think（IMO金牌）取得的65.7%的先前最佳结果。完整排行榜见https://imobench.github.io/。）。类似地，在博士级练习的内部基准（FutureMath Basic）上，Aletheia也展示了最优性能。值得注意的是，本文介绍并反思了Aletheia与数学家合作取得的首批数学研究论文²²²详见我们的项目页面https://github.com/google-deepmind/superhuman/tree/main/aletheia，包含详细提示和模型输出。

items.en: Reliable autonomous research. A research paper [Feng2026] generated by AI without any human intervention, calculating certain structure constants in arithmetic geometry called eigenweights.
items.zh: 可靠的自主研究。一篇研究论文[Feng2026]由AI在无需任何人工干预的情况下生成，计算了算术几何中称为特征权重的某些结构常数。

items.en: AI-guided collaboration. A research paper [LeeSeo2026] demonstrating human-AI collaboration in proving bounds on systems of interacting particles called independent sets.
items.zh: AI引导的协作。一篇研究论文[LeeSeo2026]展示了人机协作证明称为独立集的相互作用粒子系统的界限。

items.en: An extensive semi-autonomous evaluation on the Erdős problems. A report erdos on a systematic deployment of our agent on the 700 open problems on Bloom’s Erdős Conjectures database333https://www.erdosproblems.com/., including four open questions of Erdős that it resolves, verified semi-autonomously using both AI grading and human experts. Beyond solving Erdős-1051 completely, our model drove a generalization which resulted in a research paper [BKKKZ].
items.zh: 对Erdős问题的广泛半自主评估。一份关于在Bloom的Erdős猜想数据库³³³https://www.erdosproblems.com/的700个开放问题上系统部署我们智能体的报告，包括它解决的四个Erdős开放问题，并通过AI评分和人类专家半自主验证。除了完全解决Erdős-1051，我们的模型还推动了一个泛化，从而产生了一篇研究论文[BKKKZ]。

items.en: In addition, the agent contributed intermediate propositions on two further research papers, [FYZ4] and [ACGKMP], improving prior proofs by the human authors.
items.zh: 此外，该智能体为另外两篇研究论文[FYZ4]和[ACGKMP]贡献了中间命题，改进了人类作者先前的证明。

items.en: Given the current pace of improvement, it seems inevitable that AI will play an increasing role in mathematics research. However, for the vast majority of mathematics research results, only a few experts are equipped to properly evaluate their novelty and significance. This evaluation gap has enabled misinformation about AI-generated mathematics to spread unchecked in popular media. With a view towards transparent communication of future developments to the public, we propose codifying representations of AI-generated mathematics according to a standard taxonomy of “Autonomous Mathematics Research Levels”, analogous to the SAE Levels of Vehicle Autonomy.
items.zh: 鉴于当前的改进速度，AI在数学研究中扮演越来越重要的角色似乎不可避免。然而，对于绝大多数数学研究成果，只有少数专家能够正确评估其新颖性和重要性。这种评估差距使得关于AI生成数学的错误信息在流行媒体中不受控制地传播。为了向公众透明地传达未来发展，我们建议根据“自主数学研究水平”的标准分类法对AI生成的数学进行编码表示，类似于SAE车辆自主性水平。

items.en: While the ultimate specifics of such a taxonomy should be left to the mathematical community, we suggest features that would be informative, such as separate axes for representing the mathematical significance and the degree of AI contribution. For concreteness, we sketch one possible taxonomy in §5.1, and use it to organize our own AI-assisted mathematics results, as displayed in Table 1. In particular, this contextualizes the ‘Open’ (according to ErdosProblems.com) Erdős problems that we solved, most of which turned out—despite being open for several decades—to be quite elementary.444In fact, one such ‘Open’ problem (Erdős-397, posed in 1980) was eventually discovered to be nearly identical to a problem on the 2012 Team Selection Test for the Chinese IMO team AoPS12, a (challenging) exam for high school students; we exclude it from our classification since we consider the solution to be already in the literature. It also clarifies that our autonomous results, while milestones for artificial intelligence, are not claimed to be “major advances” for mathematics (as defined in §5.1).
items.zh: 虽然这种分类法的最终具体细节应由数学界决定，但我们建议提供信息丰富的特征，例如分别表示数学重要性和AI贡献程度的轴。为具体起见，我们在§5.1中勾勒了一种可能的分类法，并用它来组织我们自己的AI辅助数学结果，如表1所示。特别是，这为我们解决的“开放”（根据ErdosProblems.com）Erdős问题提供了背景，其中大多数尽管开放了几十年，结果证明是相当初等的。⁴⁴⁴事实上，其中一个“开放”问题（Erdős-397，提出于1980年）最终被发现与2012年中国IMO队选拔考试（AoPS12）中的一个问题几乎相同，这是一场（具有挑战性的）高中生考试；我们将其排除在分类之外，因为我们认为该解决方案已在文献中。它还澄清了我们的自主结果，虽然是人工智能的里程碑，但并未声称是数学的“重大进展”（如§5.1所定义）。

full.sec: 2 Aletheia agent: From Olympiads to Research-level Mathematics
full.secZh: 2 Aletheia智能体：从奥赛到研究级数学

items.en: This section outlines the methodological framework utilized in the development of agents capable of addressing research-grade mathematics. There are many challenges involved in transferring capabilities from contest problem-solving to mathematics research. While IMO problems require ingenuity, their solutions usually span only a few pages and rely only on standard theorems from the high school curriculum. By contrast, research-level mathematics draws on advanced techniques from vast literature, with papers often spanning dozens of pages. Human mathematicians, even IMO medalists, usually take many years of postgraduate study to reach the frontier of mathematical research. While foundation models possess a large knowledge base from pretraining, their understanding of advanced subjects remains superficial due to data scarcity, and they are also prone to hallucinations.
items.zh: 本节概述了用于开发能够处理研究级数学的智能体的方法论框架。将能力从竞赛问题解决转移到数学研究面临许多挑战。虽然IMO问题需要独创性，但其解决方案通常只有几页，且仅依赖于高中课程中的标准定理。相比之下，研究级数学依赖于大量文献中的先进技术，论文通常长达数十页。人类数学家，即使是IMO奖牌获得者，通常需要多年的研究生学习才能达到数学研究的前沿。虽然基础模型通过预训练拥有庞大的知识库，但由于数据稀缺，它们对高级主题的理解仍然肤浅，并且容易产生幻觉。

items.capEn: Figure 1: Overview of Aletheia, a math research agent powered by Deep Think that can iteratively generate, verify, and revise for research-level math problems.
items.capZh: 图1：Aletheia概述，一个由Deep Think驱动的数学研究智能体，能够为研究级数学问题迭代生成、验证和修正。

items.en: To address these issues, we built a math research agent, internally codenamed Aletheia, on top of Gemini Deep Think. Aletheia include three subagents, a (solution) Generator, a Verifier, and a Reviser that interact continuously until a solution is found that the Verifier approves, or until the attempts reach a preset (hyperparameter) limit. The subagent orchestration is sketched in Figure 1. In turn, each of the three subagents involves internal orchestration of calls to a Gemini base model.
items.zh: 为了解决这些问题，我们在Gemini Deep Think之上构建了一个数学研究智能体，内部代号为Aletheia。Aletheia包含三个子智能体：（解决方案）生成器、验证器和修正器，它们持续交互，直到找到验证器认可的解决方案，或尝试次数达到预设（超参数）限制。子智能体的编排如图1所示。反过来，每个子智能体都涉及对Gemini基础模型调用的内部编排。

items.en: In contrast to AlphaGeometry Trinh2024-alphageometry-1, chervonyi2025goldmedalistperformancesolvingolympiad-alphageometry2 and AlphaProof Hubert2025, that use formal language, Aletheia operates end-to-end in natural language. At the same time, designs similar to Aletheia have been demonstrated elsewhere. For example, Huang–Yang huang2025winninggoldimo2025 showed that a manually crafted solver-verifier harness could boost GPT-5, Gemini 2.5 Pro, or Grok 4 to Gold Medal performance on the 2025 IMO. Another example is the FullProof system555The authors were not aware of this work until after the results of this paper were collected. built by Salafatinos bryan2026motivic, which is another mathematics specific research agent incorporating a separate informal verifier mechanism.
items.zh: 与使用形式语言的AlphaGeometry（Trinh2024-alphageometry-1, chervonyi2025goldmedalistperformancesolvingolympiad-alphageometry2）和AlphaProof（Hubert2025）不同，Aletheia以自然语言端到端运行。同时，类似Aletheia的设计已在其他地方得到展示。例如，Huang–Yang（huang2025winninggoldimo2025）表明，手动构建的求解器-验证器框架可以将GPT-5、Gemini 2.5 Pro或Grok 4提升至2025年IMO的金牌表现。另一个例子是Salafatinos（bryan2026motivic）构建的FullProof系统⁵⁵⁵作者在本文结果收集完成后才知晓这项工作。，这是另一个数学专用研究智能体，包含独立的非形式验证器机制。

full.sec: 2.1 Scaling Laws and the Evolution of Deep Think
full.secZh: 2.1 规模定律与深度思考的演进

items.en: We first discuss some empirical findings about inference time scaling laws for Olympiad-level and PhD-level problems, which underlie the development of Deep Think. To investigate the inference scaling behavior, we evaluated different advanced versions of Gemini Deep Think deepmind:gemini2p5deepthink. Since Deep Think leverages parallel thinking to explore multiple ideas at once, we were able to flexibly adjust the amount of compute spent at inference time. To ensure that results were not cherry-picked, we ran the model exactly once for each problem at each compute scale, with tool use disabled, and the outputs were carefully graded according to the grading guideline.
items.zh: 我们首先讨论关于奥林匹克级和博士级问题的推理时缩放定律的一些实证发现，这些发现是Deep Think发展的基础。为了研究推理缩放行为，我们评估了不同高级版本的Gemini Deep Think（deepmind:gemini2p5deepthink）。由于Deep Think利用并行思维同时探索多个想法，我们能够灵活调整推理时花费的算力量。为确保结果不是精心挑选的，我们在每个算力规模下对每个问题仅运行模型一次，禁用工具使用，并根据评分指南仔细评分。

items.en: For Olympiad level, we leveraged the the advanced subset of IMO-ProofBench luong2025robustmathematicalreasoning which consists of 30 problems similar in difficulty and problem style to those found at the IMO. This was the benchmark used during our IMO 2025 preparation. We observed that the inference-time compute could be increased by orders of magnitude while yielding substantial gains in accuracy, as graded by human experts, before eventually plateauing (Figure 2(a)). Ultimately, an advanced version of Gemini Deep Think achieved a gold-medal standard at the IMO in July 2025, perfectly solving five out of six problems imo-gold.
items.zh: 对于奥林匹克级，我们利用了IMO-ProofBench（luong2025robustmathematicalreasoning）的高级子集，该子集包含30个难度和问题风格与IMO相似的问题。这是我们在2025年IMO准备期间使用的基准。我们观察到，推理时算力可以增加几个数量级，同时在准确性上产生显著提升（由人类专家评分），最终趋于平稳（图2(a)）。最终，Gemini Deep Think的高级版本在2025年7月达到了IMO金牌标准，完美解决了六个问题中的五个（imo-gold）。

items.en: Building upon the key recipes developed for the IMO-gold model and incorporating a suite of novel technical improvements, we trained a stronger model, Gemini Deep Think (advanced version, Jan 2026), that significantly improved inference efficiency. The compute required to achieve equivalent performance on IMO-ProofBench was reduced by approximately two orders of magnitude (100x). Specifically, utilizing extreme scale and simple prompting, Gemini Deep Think (advanced version, Jan 2026), without internet access, demonstrated the potential to solve exceptionally difficult reasoning tasks such as IMO 2025 Problem 6 (see Appendix B), that our previous IMO-gold model failed to solve. Notably, the latest model also solved IMO 2024 Problem 3 with a minor mistake (achieved at 272^{7} scale) and completely solved Problem 5 (at 282^{8} scale) (see Appendix C)666We caution that the model’s knowledge cutoff falls between IMO 2024 and IMO 2025, implying possible exposure to the IMO 2024 problems. Additionally, as IMO 2025 has been public for six months, we do not claim the problem was strictly unseen. We report these findings to transparently demonstrate the potential of our new Gemini Deep Think model. .
items.zh: 基于为IMO金牌模型开发的关键配方，并结合一系列新颖的技术改进，我们训练了一个更强的模型Gemini Deep Think（高级版，2026年1月），显著提高了推理效率。在IMO-ProofBench上达到同等性能所需的算力减少了大约两个数量级（100倍）。具体来说，利用极端规模和简单提示，Gemini Deep Think（高级版，2026年1月）在无互联网访问的情况下，展示了解决极其困难的推理任务（如IMO 2025问题6，见附录B）的潜力，而我们之前的IMO金牌模型未能解决该问题。值得注意的是，最新模型还解决了IMO 2024问题3（有轻微错误，在2^7规模下实现）并完全解决了问题5（在2^8规模下）（见附录C）⁶⁶⁶我们提醒，模型的知识截止日期介于IMO 2024和IMO 2025之间，意味着可能接触过IMO 2024问题。此外，由于IMO 2025已公开六个月，我们不声称该问题严格未见过。我们报告这些发现是为了透明地展示我们新Gemini Deep Think模型的潜力。。

items.en: To further assess the readiness of our models for research-grade mathematics, we evaluated on FutureMath Basic, an internal benchmark designed to test PhD-level mathematics knowledge. We give an example of a FutureMath benchmark problem in appendix §A. Figure 2(b) demonstrated a similar scaling law for PhD-level problems, though with significantly lower accuracy compared to the competition problems. Corroborating these quantitative results, expert mathematicians who assessed the model noted that its susceptibility to mistakes and hallucinations inhibited its effectiveness on the longer, more complex reasoning required for research. With these findings, we concluded that inference-time scaling alone would not be sufficient, and we shifted our focus towards further improving and adapting our systems for research-level math.
items.zh: 为了进一步评估我们的模型在研究级数学上的准备情况，我们在FutureMath Basic上进行了评估，这是一个旨在测试博士级数学知识的内部基准。我们在附录§A中给出了一个FutureMath基准问题的示例。图2(b)展示了博士级问题的类似缩放定律，尽管准确率显著低于竞赛问题。与这些定量结果一致，评估模型的数学专家指出，其容易出错和产生幻觉的倾向抑制了其在研究所需的更长、更复杂推理中的有效性。基于这些发现，我们得出结论，仅靠推理时缩放是不够的，我们将重点转向进一步改进和调整我们的系统以适应研究级数学。

full.sec: 2.2 Developing Agentic Harnesses for Research-Level Math
full.secZh: 2.2 为研究级数学开发智能体框架

items.en: The design of Aletheia is motivated by an empirical observation: decoupling a reasoning model’s final output from its intermediate thinking tokens, and adding well-chosen prompt scaffolding, enables the model to recognize flaws it initially overlooked during generation. One possible explanation for this phenomenon is that the training process incentivizes the model to guess or bluff. Another hypothesis is that an extended thinking trace might act as misleading “supporting” context, artificially inflating the conditional probability of an erroneous solution.
items.zh: Aletheia 的设计源于一个经验观察：将推理模型的最终输出与其中间思考令牌解耦，并添加精心选择的提示脚手架，使模型能够识别其在生成过程中最初忽略的缺陷。这种现象的一种可能解释是，训练过程激励模型进行猜测或虚张声势。另一种假设是，扩展的思考轨迹可能充当误导性的“支持”上下文，人为地提高了错误解的条件概率。

items.en: Regardless of the underlying mechanism, we observed that explicitly separating out the verification step is effective in practice. Figure 2 depicts Aletheia’s performance relative to Gemini Deep Think, the agent that achieved the 2025 IMO Gold Medal standard imo-gold. Due to its dynamic nature, Aletheia’s total inference compute cannot be precisely controlled, but the results indicate that Aletheia outperforms Deep Think at similar (and indeed, larger) compute scales. On IMO-ProofBench Advanced, Aletheia achieved a 95.1% overall score, surpassing Deep Think across all tested compute scales using the same base model (Figure 2(a)). Moreover, on the 29 out of 30 problems where Aletheia actually returned a solution, its conditional accuracy reached 98.3%. On the FutureMath Basic (Figure 2(b)), Aletheia again outperformed Deep Think at all compute scales on the same base model. Notably, it returned solutions for fewer than 60% of these problems, and its conditional accuracy on the answered subset exceeded 82%. Thanks to its verification mechanism, Aletheia often admits failure to solve a problem, a key feature that improved the efficiency of our human-AI research collaborations.
items.zh: 无论底层机制如何，我们观察到明确分离验证步骤在实践中是有效的。图 2 展示了 Aletheia 相对于 Gemini Deep Think（达到 2025 年 IMO 金牌标准 imo-gold 的智能体）的性能。由于其动态特性，Aletheia 的总推理算力无法精确控制，但结果表明，在相似（甚至更大）的算力规模下，Aletheia 优于 Deep Think。在 IMO-ProofBench Advanced 上，Aletheia 获得了 95.1% 的总体分数，在使用相同基础模型的所有测试算力规模上均超越了 Deep Think（图 2(a)）。此外，在 Aletheia 实际返回解的 30 个问题中的 29 个上，其条件准确率达到 98.3%。在 FutureMath Basic（图 2(b)）上，Aletheia 在使用相同基础模型的所有算力规模上再次优于 Deep Think。值得注意的是，它在不到 60% 的问题上返回了解，并且在已回答子集上的条件准确率超过 82%。得益于其验证机制，Aletheia 经常承认无法解决问题，这一关键特性提高了我们人机协作研究的效率。

items.en: Beyond its foundational natural language verifier, Aletheia relies heavily on tool use to navigate the complexities of mathematical research. We find that a foundation model operating without such tools is prone to frequent hallucinations—primarily in the form of spurious citations (see Figure 3 for an example), and to a lesser extent, computational inaccuracies. In contrast to the structured, bounded nature of IMO problems, mathematical research requires synthesizing published literature to address broader, open-ended questions. We observed that simply granting internet access is insufficient; however, when our model was extensively trained for tool use, the integration of Google Search and web browsing led to a substantial reduction in obvious citation hallucinations with fictitious titles and/or authors. This shifted citation errors to subtler hallucinations, for example where the reference exists but its results are not quoted properly (as illustrated in Figure 4).
items.zh: 除了其基础的自然语言验证器之外，Aletheia 还严重依赖工具使用来应对数学研究的复杂性。我们发现，没有此类工具的基础模型容易频繁产生幻觉——主要表现为虚假引用（参见图 3 的示例），以及较小程度的计算不准确。与 IMO 问题的结构化、有界性质不同，数学研究需要综合已发表文献来回答更广泛、开放的问题。我们观察到，仅提供互联网访问是不够的；然而，当我们的模型经过广泛的工具使用训练后，集成 Google 搜索和网页浏览显著减少了带有虚构标题和/或作者的明显引用幻觉。这将引用错误转变为更微妙的幻觉，例如参考文献存在但其结果未被正确引用（如图 4 所示）。

items.en: Perhaps unexpectedly, our results indicate that the integration of Python as a tool yielded only marginal improvements in mitigating computational hallucinations. This suggests that the baseline proficiency of Gemini in these specific tasks is already high; consequently, further performance gains may require more specialized or sophisticated tools beyond standard code execution.
items.zh: 也许出乎意料的是，我们的结果表明，将 Python 作为工具集成在减轻计算幻觉方面仅带来了微小的改进。这表明 Gemini 在这些特定任务中的基线能力已经很高；因此，进一步的性能提升可能需要比标准代码执行更专业或更复杂的工具。

items.en: Below we will briefly summarize the papers with substantial contributions from Aletheia that have already been publicly released. Due to timing, these results were obtained by Aletheia with a base model that was intermediate between the two base models depicted in Figure 2.
items.zh: 下面我们将简要总结已经公开发布的、由 Aletheia 做出重大贡献的论文。由于时间关系，这些结果是 Aletheia 使用介于图 2 中两个基础模型之间的中间基础模型获得的。

items.en: To be clear, the final versions of these papers were all written by human authors, starting from Aletheia’s outputs. This was due to the principle held by the human authors involved that research papers should be authored exclusively by humans, even when AI contributions reach a level that would traditionally merit co-authorship. The primary reason is that authorship of a mathematics paper implies accountability for all of its contents, and this is a responsibility that only humans can bear. We emphasize that beyond mathematical correctness, authors also assume accountability for important aspects of exposition such as adequacy and accuracy of attributions, which cannot be guaranteed by formal verification.
items.zh: 需要明确的是，这些论文的最终版本均由人类作者从 Aletheia 的输出开始撰写。这是因为相关人类作者坚持认为，研究论文应仅由人类撰写，即使 AI 的贡献达到了传统上值得合著的水平。主要原因是，数学论文的作者身份意味着对其所有内容负责，而这是只有人类才能承担的责任。我们强调，除了数学正确性之外，作者还需对阐述的重要方面（如引用的充分性和准确性）负责，而这些无法通过形式化验证来保证。

full.secZh: 3.1 里程碑A：可靠的自主研究

items.en: The paper Eigenweights for Arithmetic Hirzebruch Proportionality [Feng2026] resolves a problem that arose in the work of Feng–Yun–Zhang [FYZ4] investigating an extension of the celebrated Hirzebruch Proportionality Principle [Hir58].
items.zh: 论文《Eigenweights for Arithmetic Hirzebruch Proportionality》[Feng2026] 解决了 Feng–Yun–Zhang [FYZ4] 工作中出现的一个问题，该工作研究了著名的 Hirzebruch 比例原理 [Hir58] 的推广。

items.en: The backdrop. Hirzebruch’s original Proportionality Principle expresses the Chern numbers of an automorphic vector bundle on a compact locally symmetric space as a multiple of the corresponding Chern numbers on the compact dual variety, with a proportionality constant that can be interpreted as a value of the LL-function for the associated Gross motive [Gro97]. This formula was later generalized to non-compact locally symmetric spaces by Mumford [Mum77].
items.zh: 背景。Hirzebruch 最初的 Proportionality Principle 将紧致局部对称空间上自同构向量丛的陈数表示为紧致对偶簇上相应陈数的倍数，其比例常数可解释为相关 Gross 动机 [Gro97] 的 L-函数值。该公式后来由 Mumford [Mum77] 推广到非紧致局部对称空间。

items.en: Recent work by Feng–Yun–Zhang [FYZ4] studies a variant of this principle, called “Arithmetic Hirzebruch Proportionality”, relating the “arithmetic volume” of Chern classes on moduli spaces of shtukas to differential operators applied to the LL-function of Gross motives. It can be seen as a generalization of Gaitsgory–Lurie’s resolution of Weil’s Tamagawa Number Conjecture [GL14]. The precise differential operator appearing in Arithmetic Hirzebruch Proportionality is governed by certain fundamental structure constants called eigenweights. In [FYZ4], the authors calculated some examples of eigenweights, but did not know how to determine all of them in closed form.
items.zh: Feng–Yun–Zhang [FYZ4] 近期的工作研究了该原理的一个变体，称为“Arithmetic Hirzebruch Proportionality”，将 shtukas 模空间上陈类的“算术体积”与应用于 Gross 动机 L-函数的微分算子联系起来。它可以被视为 Gaitsgory–Lurie 对 Weil Tamagawa 数猜想 [GL14] 的推广。Arithmetic Hirzebruch Proportionality 中出现的精确微分算子由某些称为 eigenweights 的基本结构常数控制。在 [FYZ4] 中，作者计算了一些 eigenweights 的示例，但不知道如何以封闭形式确定所有 eigenweights。

items.en: The story of the paper. In fact, this project began almost incidentally, while tracking partial progress on a problem that was submitted to an internal benchmark: the computation of a particular family of eigenweights (whose answer was already known to Feng–Yun–Zhang). Initially no model could solve it, but clear progress was visible as inference time computation was scaled, until the eventual IMO Gold model found the correct answer. Upon examining the AI-generated solution, the authors of [FYZ4] preferred it over their original one, leading them to replace the original proofs in their paper.
items.zh: 论文的故事。事实上，这个项目几乎是偶然开始的，当时正在追踪提交给内部基准测试的一个问题的部分进展：计算一个特定的 eigenweights 族（其答案 Feng–Yun–Zhang 已经知道）。最初没有模型能解决，但随着推理时间算力的扩展，进展明显，直到最终的 IMO 金牌模型找到了正确答案。在检查 AI 生成的解后，[FYZ4] 的作者认为它优于他们原来的解，因此用其替换了论文中的原始证明。

items.en: This success encouraged us to task Aletheia with calculating the eigenweights in general. Without any human intervention, Aletheia found an elegant way to do this, using techniques from a different field of mathematics777Namely, algebraic combinatorics. unfamiliar to the authors of [FYZ4], and in the process resolved several questions left open in [FYZ4]. This is the subject of the paper [Feng2026], which is notable in that all of the mathematical content is fully generated by AI. While our ultimate goal is to empower human mathematicians with AI tools, we note the ability to generate correct results without human intervention as an important milestone for reliability and trustworthiness of natural language models.
items.zh: 这一成功鼓励我们让 Aletheia 计算一般的 eigenweights。在没有任何人工干预的情况下，Aletheia 找到了一种优雅的方法，使用了 [FYZ4] 作者不熟悉的另一个数学领域（即代数组合学）的技术，并在此过程中解决了 [FYZ4] 中留下的几个开放问题。这就是论文 [Feng2026] 的主题，值得注意的是，其中所有数学内容完全由 AI 生成。虽然我们的最终目标是用 AI 工具赋能人类数学家，但我们注意到，在没有人工干预的情况下生成正确结果的能力，是自然语言模型可靠性和可信度的重要里程碑。

full.sec: 3.2 Milestone B: Lower Bounds for Multivariate Independence Polynomials
full.secZh: 3.2 里程碑B：多元独立多项式的下界

items.en: The paper Lower bounds for multivariate independence polynomials and their generalisations [LeeSeo2026] establishes new mathematical inequalities that link the world of physics with the logic of discrete mathematics.
items.zh: 论文《Lower bounds for multivariate independence polynomials and their generalisations》[LeeSeo2026] 建立了新的数学不等式，将物理世界与离散数学的逻辑联系起来。

items.en: The backdrop. In physics, scientists often model how gas molecules occupy a space by imagining them as points on a network. Because these molecules naturally repel one another, no two molecules can occupy “neighboring” spots that are directly connected. Mathematicians call these valid arrangements independent sets, and they are a cornerstone of understanding complex systems in both physics and mathematics.
items.zh: 背景。在物理学中，科学家通常通过将气体分子想象为网络上的点来模拟它们如何占据空间。由于这些分子自然相互排斥，没有两个分子可以占据直接连接的“相邻”位置。数学家称这些有效排列为独立集，它们是理解物理和数学中复杂系统的基石。

items.en: The story of the paper. Joonkyung Lee and Jaehyeon Seo first used Gemini 2.5 Deep Think to prove the key inequality needed to obtain a further generalization of a well-known result by Sah–Sawhney–Stoner–Zhao [sah2019number] on the lower bound for the number of (weighted) independent sets.888Other models were also able to solve the problem: Gemini Deep Think, Aletheia, and—with some back and forth—GPT-5 Pro. Encouraged by this success, they tackled an even harder problem: modeling systems where two different types of molecules interact at the same time, such that different types do not repel each other. For this deeper challenge, Aletheia provided999Other models were tasked with the same prompt, but Aletheia’s output was the only one that the authors found useful. a high-level roadmap of insightful ideas that the authors then turned into a complete, rigorous proof.
items.zh: 论文的故事。Joonkyung Lee 和 Jaehyeon Seo 首先使用 Gemini 2.5 Deep Think 证明了关键不等式，从而进一步推广了 Sah–Sawhney–Stoner–Zhao [sah2019number] 关于（加权）独立集数量下界的著名结果。受到这一成功的鼓舞，他们着手解决一个更难的问题：对两种不同类型分子同时相互作用的系统进行建模，其中不同类型之间不相互排斥。对于这一更深层次的挑战，Aletheia 提供了一个高层次的有洞察力的思路路线图，作者随后将其转化为完整、严谨的证明。

items.en: The interesting feature here is that the workflow was somewhat reversed from the usual narrative of human-AI collaboration. Typical human-AI workflows involve decomposing a project into granular technical queries for the model to resolve. But in this project, Aletheia actually gave the “bigger picture” strategy for a deep result–such as suggesting the use of specific “dual sets”—leaving the human authors to fill in the rigorous execution. In some parts, the authors retained only the statements provided by the AI and produced the proofs independently, as if they were inspired by the model’s vision.
items.zh: 这里有趣的特点是，工作流程与通常的人机协作叙述有些相反。典型的人机协作工作流程涉及将项目分解为细粒度的技术查询供模型解决。但在这个项目中，Aletheia 实际上为深层结果提供了“大局”策略——例如建议使用特定的“对偶集”——而人类作者则负责填充严谨的执行细节。在某些部分，作者仅保留了 AI 提供的陈述，并独立完成了证明，仿佛受到了模型愿景的启发。

items.en: Erdős was a prolific Hungarian mathematician who left a vast legacy of papers and unsolved conjectures. In 2023, Thomas Bloom launched ErdosProblems.com, a centralized repository designed to catalog these conjectures and track their resolutions. At the time of this writing, the database tracks 1,179 problems, with 483 (41%) classified as solved. We stress, however, that the “Open” status of a problem in this database does not always reflect the true state of the literature.
items.zh: Erdős 是一位多产的匈牙利数学家，留下了大量论文和未解决的猜想。2023 年，Thomas Bloom 推出了 ErdosProblems.com，这是一个集中式存储库，旨在编录这些猜想并追踪其解决情况。截至撰写本文时，该数据库追踪了 1,179 个问题，其中 483 个（41%）被归类为已解决。然而，我们强调，数据库中问题的“开放”状态并不总是反映文献的真实状态。

items.en: Recently, AI managed to solve a few problems marked “Open” in Bloom’s database. While some of these solutions were later discovered to already exist in the literature, others appear to be genuinely novel. To systematically document these advancements, Terence Tao tao2026erdosai established a community wiki dedicated to tracking AI-assisted progress on Erdős’s conjectures.
items.zh: 最近，AI 成功解决了 Bloom 数据库中标记为“开放”的几个问题。虽然其中一些解后来被发现已存在于文献中，但其他解似乎是真正新颖的。为了系统记录这些进展，Terence Tao tao2026erdosai 建立了一个社区维基，专门追踪 AI 辅助下对 Erdős 猜想的进展。

items.en: Because only success cases tend to be reported in public forums, these results do not provide a complete picture of AI capability. In order to understand this better, we initiated a case study on the Erdős problems in early December, 2025. From December 2–9, we deployed Aletheia against the 700 Erdős problems then marked as “Open” in Bloom’s database. Aletheia’s informal verifier mechanism streamlined the initial pool of candidates: of the 700 original prompts, the model returned 212 responses as potentially correct. These candidates were then evaluated by a team of mathematicians. Most were not experts in the relevant problem domain, so we prioritized narrowing the pool of candidate solutions quickly (possibly at the cost of making noisier judgments) to a manageable size for our smaller core of domain experts. This step was essentially completed by December 21. Then our internal domain experts vetted the solutions carefully, consulting external experts when correctness was ascertained but novelty was unclear. In some cases, minor inaccuracies in the solutions were corrected.
items.zh: 由于只有成功案例往往在公共论坛中被报道，这些结果并不能提供 AI 能力的完整图景。为了更好地理解这一点，我们于 2025 年 12 月初启动了关于 Erdős 问题的案例研究。从 12 月 2 日到 9 日，我们针对 Bloom 数据库中当时标记为“开放”的 700 个 Erdős 问题部署了 Aletheia。Aletheia 的非正式验证器机制精简了初始候选池：在 700 个原始提示中，模型返回了 212 个响应作为可能正确的候选。然后由一组数学家对这些候选进行评估。大多数数学家并非相关问题的专家，因此我们优先快速缩小候选解池（可能以做出更嘈杂的判断为代价），使其达到我们较小的核心领域专家可管理的规模。这一步基本上在 12 月 21 日完成。然后我们的内部领域专家仔细审查了解，并在正确性确定但新颖性不明确时咨询了外部专家。在某些情况下，解中的小错误被纠正。

items.en: Our ultimate findings were that 63 solutions were technically correct, but only 13 solutions correctly addressed the intended problem statement (either by invoking the literature, or by a novel argument). The remaining 50 of Aletheia’s correct solutions were technically valid but mathematically vacuous, because the problem statements were interpreted in a way that did not capture Erdős’s intent, often (but not always) leading to trivial solutions. Finally, 12 of the responses were marked ambiguous, for example due to open-endedness of the question itself. In summary, out of the 200 solution candidates that we were definitely able to mark correct or incorrect, 137 (68.5%68.5\%) of the responses were fundamentally flawed, while 63 (31.5%31.5\%) of the responses were technically correct, of which only 13 (6.5%6.5\%) were meaningfully correct. These 13 meaningfully correct results clustered naturally into four categories which we felt should be distinguished; see Table 2 for a summary.
items.zh: 我们的最终发现是，63 个解在技术上是正确的，但只有 13 个解正确解决了预期的问题陈述（要么通过引用文献，要么通过新颖的论证）。其余 50 个 Aletheia 的正确解在技术上是有效的，但在数学上是空洞的，因为问题陈述的解释方式没有捕捉到 Erdős 的意图，通常（但并非总是）导致平凡解。最后，12 个响应被标记为模糊，例如由于问题本身的开放性。总之，在我们能够明确标记正确或错误的 200 个候选解中，137 个（68.5%）响应存在根本缺陷，而 63 个（31.5%）响应在技术上是正确的，其中只有 13 个（6.5%）是有意义的正确。这 13 个有意义的正确结果自然地聚类为四个我们认为应该区分的类别；总结见表 2。

items.en: On these problems, Aletheia found the first correct solution (as far as we can tell) in a mathematically substantive way. These include Erdős-652 and Erdős-1051.
items.zh: 在这些问题上，Aletheia 以数学上实质性的方式找到了第一个正确解（据我们所知）。这些包括 Erdős-652 和 Erdős-1051。

items.en: On these problems, there were multiple questions and Aletheia found the first correct solution to one of the questions. These include Erdős-654, and Erdős-1040.
items.zh: 在这些问题上，存在多个问题，Aletheia 找到了其中一个问题的第一个正确解。这些问题包括 Erdős-654 和 Erdős-1040。

items.en: On these problems, Aletheia found a correct solution, but human auditors subsequently found an independent solution already in the literature. These include Erdős-397, Erdős-659, Erdős-935, and Erdős-1089. The solutions appear to have been independently rediscovered by our model: we scanned the logs of Aletheia’s reasoning trace to ensure that the solution was not pulled directly from the literature solution.101010It is of course possible that the solution was indirectly ingested from the literature solution, either implicitly through intermediate sources or during pretraining. This highlights a new danger that accompanies AI-generated mathematics: it is susceptible to “subconscious plagiarism” by reproducing knowledge acquired during pretraining, without attribution.
items.zh: 在这些问题上，Aletheia 找到了一个正确解，但随后人类审计员在文献中发现了一个独立的已有解。这些问题包括 Erdős-397、Erdős-659、Erdős-935 和 Erdős-1089。这些解似乎是由我们的模型独立重新发现的：我们扫描了 Aletheia 推理轨迹的日志，以确保该解并非直接取自文献中的解。101010当然，该解可能间接来自文献解，要么通过中间来源隐式获取，要么在预训练期间获取。这凸显了 AI 生成数学的一个新危险：它容易通过复现预训练期间获得的知识而进行“潜意识抄袭”，且不注明出处。

items.en: On these problems, Aletheia found that a solution was already explicitly in the literature, despite the problem being marked “Open” on Bloom’s website at the time of model deployment. These include Erdős-333, Erdős-591, Erdős-705, Erdős-992, Erdős-1105.
items.zh: 在这些问题上，Aletheia 发现尽管在模型部署时 Bloom 网站上将该问题标记为“开放”，但文献中已明确存在一个解。这些问题包括 Erdős-333、Erdős-591、Erdős-705、Erdős-992 和 Erdős-1105。

items.en: To be clear, we make no claims of novelty for the latter two categories. The ‘4’ autonomous solutions cited above refer to Erdős-652, Erdős-654, Erdős-935, Erdős-1040, and Erdős-1051. In the estimation of our experts, none of the four individually rises to the level of a research paper. The solution to Erdős-1051 was generalized further, in a collaborative effort by Aletheia together with human mathematicians and Gemini Deep Think, to produce the research paper [BKKKZ].
items.zh: 需要明确的是，我们对后两类不作新颖性声明。上述‘4’个自主解指的是 Erdős-652、Erdős-654、Erdős-935、Erdős-1040 和 Erdős-1051。据我们的专家评估，这四个解中没有一个单独达到研究论文的水平。对 Erdős-1051 的解，通过 Aletheia 与人类数学家及 Gemini Deep Think 的合作努力，进一步推广，产生了研究论文 [BKKKZ]。

items.en: Contextualizing the results. A disclaimer is necessary regarding the novelty of these results on Erdős problems. While we made considerable efforts to review the literature, it is certainly possible that we missed earlier solutions to these problems by human mathematicians. Therefore, our initial classification into categories is, at best, an upper bound on novelty. It is subject to revision after further investigation by the public. Indeed, previous AI-assisted work on Erdős problems 1026, 397, 333, and 281 was discovered, after initial announcements of novelty, be redundant with the literature111111For Erdős-281, we note that the AI solution is distinct from the previously existing literature solution.. To the outside observer, this may present a misleading impression of mathematics research: in the authors’ experience, it is very unusual for human-generated results to be redundant in this manner (in the modern era of communication). One reason why it seems to be happening so frequently with AI-generated work on Erdős problems is that the solutions are so simple that they would not necessarily attract attention if they originated from humans. For instance, Erdős-1089 is answered by an offhand remark in a 1981 paper Bannai81, where the authors seemed unaware that they had resolved an Erdős problem. Our takeaway from this experience is that many open Erdős problems remained unresolved out of obscurity rather than difficulty. We stress that the mathematical significance of such resolutions can only be accurately evaluated by expert mathematicians, even if the correctness can be ascertained by non-mathematicians or formal verifiers. See erdos for further discussion.
items.zh: 对结果的背景说明。关于这些 Erdős 问题结果的新颖性，有必要进行免责声明。尽管我们付出了大量努力来审查文献，但很可能遗漏了人类数学家对这些问题的早期解。因此，我们最初的分类充其量只是新颖性的上界。在公众进一步调查后，该分类可能会被修正。事实上，先前关于 Erdős 问题 1026、397、333 和 281 的 AI 辅助工作，在最初宣布新颖性后，被发现与文献重复111111对于 Erdős-281，我们注意到 AI 解与先前存在的文献解是不同的。。对于外部观察者来说，这可能会对数学研究产生误导性印象：根据作者的经验，人类生成的结果以这种方式重复是非常罕见的（在现代通信时代）。之所以在 AI 生成的 Erdős 问题工作中频繁出现这种情况，一个原因是这些解非常简单，如果源自人类，不一定会引起注意。例如，Erdős-1089 由 1981 年 Bannai81 论文中的一句随口评论回答，作者似乎并未意识到他们解决了一个 Erdős 问题。我们从这一经验中得到的启示是，许多开放的 Erdős 问题之所以未解决，更多是由于其默默无闻而非难度。我们强调，此类解决方法的数学重要性只能由专家数学家准确评估，即使非数学家或形式验证者可以确认其正确性。更多讨论见 erdos。

items.en: The paper Strongly Polynomial Policy Iteration for L∞L_{\infty} Robust MDPs by Asadi–Chatterjee–Goharshady–Karrabi–Montaseri–Pagano [ACGKMP] proves complexity bounds for certain algorithms arising in Machine Learning and Game Theory called “Robust Markov Decision Processes (MDPs)”. For this paper, Aletheia provided an improvement to a crucial mathematical ingredient.
items.zh: Asadi–Chatterjee–Goharshady–Karrabi–Montaseri–Pagano [ACGKMP] 的论文《Strongly Polynomial Policy Iteration for L∞L_{\infty} Robust MDPs》证明了机器学习与博弈论中称为“鲁棒马尔可夫决策过程（MDP）”的某些算法的复杂度界。对于这篇论文，Aletheia 提供了一个关键数学成分的改进。

items.en: The backdrop. This work was originally a project of Asadi–Chatterjee–Goharshady–Karrabi–Montaseri, a team of computer scientists at ISTA. They were able to prove the desired strongly-polynomial time bound on Robust MDPs conditionally on a number-theoretic assertion: that specific bounded combinations of numbers are in polynomially many dyadic intervals. They reached out to number theorists for help, and eventually the problem arrived at Pagano, who, after some effort, could prove the desired complexity bound using a relatively advanced result from Number Theory called Siegel’s Lemma (a tool that does not immediately appear related to the question). Pagano shared the problem with several colleagues and some of them eventually found variants of the argument leading to the same complexity bound. The result was sufficient for the intended algorithmic application, but the optimality of the bounds was unclear.
items.zh: 背景。这项工作最初是 ISTA 计算机科学家团队 Asadi–Chatterjee–Goharshady–Karrabi–Montaseri 的一个项目。他们能够有条件地证明鲁棒 MDP 上所需的强多项式时间界，该条件是一个数论断言：特定的有界数字组合位于多项式多个二进区间内。他们向数论学家寻求帮助，最终问题传到了 Pagano 手中，经过一番努力，他能够使用一个相对高级的数论结果——Siegel 引理（一个看似与问题无关的工具）来证明所需的复杂度界。Pagano 与几位同事分享了这个问题，其中一些人最终找到了导致相同复杂度界的论证变体。该结果足以用于预期的算法应用，但界的最优性尚不清楚。

items.en: The story of the paper. Pagano also contributed the problem to an internal benchmark, where the IMO Gold version of Deep Think provided a valid solution. However, Aletheia devised an independent argument (also making creative use of Siegel’s Lemma) that achieved the best bound among all human and autonomous attempts. In particular, this significantly improved the bound established originally by Pagano and the other mathematicians. Therefore, this argument was adopted for the eventual publication.
items.zh: 论文的故事。Pagano 还将该问题贡献给了一个内部基准测试，其中 IMO 金牌版本的 Deep Think 提供了一个有效解。然而，Aletheia 设计了一个独立的论证（也创造性地使用了 Siegel 引理），在所有人类和自主尝试中取得了最佳界。特别是，这显著改进了 Pagano 和其他数学家最初建立的界。因此，该论证被用于最终的发表。

items.en: To contextualize Aletheia’s performance, we conducted ablation studies using the Gemini Deep Think agent, operating at the IMO Gold scale with the identical underlying base model. We evaluated Deep Think using the same prompts that generated the research results detailed in §3.
items.zh: 为了对 Aletheia 的性能进行背景分析，我们使用 Gemini Deep Think 智能体进行了消融研究，该智能体在 IMO 金牌规模下运行，使用相同的基础模型。我们使用生成第 3 节详细研究结果的相同提示对 Deep Think 进行了评估。

items.en: We first evaluate the 13 Erdős problems that Aletheia successfully solved, as listed in §3.3. According to human expert grading121212Natural language verification by human experts inherently involves some subjectivity; see Footnote 3 of erdos., Gemini Deep Think correctly solved 8 of these 13 problems, while operating at almost exactly twice the average compute per problem as Aletheia. These results are summarized in Table 3.
items.zh: 我们首先评估了 Aletheia 成功解决的 13 个 Erdős 问题，如第 3.3 节所列。根据人类专家评分121212人类专家的自然语言验证固有地包含一些主观性；见 erdos 的脚注 3。，Gemini Deep Think 正确解决了这 13 个问题中的 8 个，而每个问题的平均算力几乎是 Aletheia 的两倍。这些结果总结在表 3 中。

items.en: Next we compare performance on the specific prompts underlying the research papers documented in §3. As summarized in Table 4, Deep Think successfully reproduced the correct answer for [FYZ4] but failed across all three prompts for [Feng2026]. For the prompts in [LeeSeo2026], it resolved the first prompt but failed on the second and far more crucial prompt. For [BKKKZ], the interaction was too messy to replicate exactly, but Deep Think essentially succeeded. Lastly, for [ACGKMP], Deep Think derived a satisfactory upper bound, although it was not as sharp as Aletheia’s. On this set of prompts, the total compute used by the two agents turned out to be comparable. The results are summarized in Table 4.
items.zh: 接下来，我们比较了第 3 节中记录的研究论文所依据的特定提示上的性能。如表 4 总结，Deep Think 成功复现了 [FYZ4] 的正确结果，但在 [Feng2026] 的所有三个提示上均失败。对于 [LeeSeo2026] 的提示，它解决了第一个提示，但在第二个且更为关键的提示上失败。对于 [BKKKZ]，交互过于混乱无法精确复现，但 Deep Think 基本成功。最后，对于 [ACGKMP]，Deep Think 推导出了一个令人满意的上界，尽管不如 Aletheia 的界那么尖锐。在这组提示上，两个智能体使用的总算力相当。结果总结在表 4 中。

items.en: The results of this paper should not be interpreted as suggesting that AI can consistently solve research-level mathematics questions. In fact, our anecdotal experience is the opposite: success cases are rare, and an apt intuition for autonomous capabilities (and limitations) may currently be important for finding such cases. The papers [Feng2026, LeeSeo2026, ACGKMP] grew out of spontaneous positive outcomes in a wider benchmarking effort on research-level problems; for most of these problems, no autonomous progress was made.
items.zh: 本文的结果不应被解读为 AI 能够持续解决研究级数学问题。事实上，我们的轶事经验恰恰相反：成功案例很少见，目前对自主能力（和局限性）的恰当直觉可能对于发现此类案例很重要。论文 [Feng2026, LeeSeo2026, ACGKMP] 源于对研究级问题更广泛基准测试中的自发积极结果；对于大多数这些问题，没有取得自主进展。

items.en: The case study erdos on the Erdős problems offers a more quantitative picture. Table 5 illustrates the success rate on the 200 solution candidates that we were able to mark Correct or Incorrect (filtered from 700 open problems). In total, 31.5% of the solutions were technically correct under some interpretation of the question, but only 6.5% were meaningfully correct in addressing what we deemed to be the intended interpretation.
items.zh: 关于 Erdős 问题的案例研究 erdos 提供了更定量的图景。表 5 展示了我们能够标记为正确或错误的 200 个候选解（从 700 个开放问题中筛选）的成功率。总体而言，31.5% 的解在问题的某种解释下技术上正确，但只有 6.5% 在解决我们认为的预期解释方面有意义地正确。

items.en: Thus, even with its verifier mechanism, the model is still highly prone to errors compared to humans. (We note, however, that the base model used on the Erdős problems is substantially weaker than the January 2026 base model referenced in §3.) Furthermore, whenever there is room for ambiguity, the model exhibits a tendency to misinterpret the question in a way that is easiest to answer, even when such an interpretation would be obviously unintended to a human expert. This aligns with the well-known tendencies for “specification gaming” and “reward hacking” in machine learning. Finally, we reiterate that hallucination is still a common failure mode. Even with internet search capability to check references, the model tends to fabricate or misrepresent results from legitimate references in order to assert a solution (see Figure 4 for an example).
items.zh: 因此，即使有验证器机制，该模型仍然比人类更容易出错。（然而，我们注意到，用于 Erdős 问题的基础模型比第 3 节中引用的 2026 年 1 月基础模型弱得多。）此外，只要存在歧义空间，模型就倾向于以最容易回答的方式误解问题，即使这种解释对人类专家来说显然不是本意。这与机器学习中众所周知的“规范博弈”和“奖励黑客”倾向一致。最后，我们重申幻觉仍然是一个常见的失败模式。即使具备检查参考文献的互联网搜索能力，模型也倾向于捏造或歪曲合法参考文献中的结果以断言一个解（示例见图 4）。

items.en: We identify several qualitative gaps between current autonomous mathematics research and human-generated research. To date, autonomous results have been relatively brief and elementary in comparison to typical human papers. Furthermore, success cases seem to arise from clever technical manipulations or vast knowledge retrieval, rather than what mathematicians would consider to be genuine creativity, although the latter concept is admittedly subjective.
items.zh: 我们指出了当前自主数学研究与人类生成研究之间的几个定性差距。迄今为止，自主结果相对简短且初等，与典型的人类论文相比。此外，成功案例似乎源于巧妙的技术操作或广泛的知识检索，而非数学家认为的真正创造力，尽管后者概念无疑是主观的。

items.en: Mathematics provides a domain that is—at least superficially—analogous to game-playing, making it a highly attractive target for AI researchers. In mathematics there is a near-universal consensus on starting points (mathematical axioms) and atomic moves (valid mathematical deductions), and a rich landscape of objectives (precise theorems and conjectures). However, the extreme difficulty and specialization of frontier mathematics research makes performance evaluation exceptionally challenging. For a given research problem, there may be only a handful of mathematicians in the world equipped to evaluate a potential solution, and such evaluations can take tremendous effort. (For example, in mathematics the peer review process typically exceeds a year, and it is common for research papers to surpass 100 pages in length.) Even given a solution that is known to be correct, perhaps through formal verification, it could be difficult for an outside observer to distinguish a major advance from a triviality.
items.zh: 数学提供了一个——至少表面上——类似于游戏领域的领域，使其成为 AI 研究人员极具吸引力的目标。在数学中，关于起点（数学公理）和原子步骤（有效数学推理）存在近乎普遍的共识，并且有丰富的目标（精确的定理和猜想）。然而，前沿数学研究的极端难度和专业化使得性能评估异常具有挑战性。对于一个给定的研究问题，世界上可能只有少数数学家能够评估潜在解，并且此类评估可能需要巨大努力。（例如，在数学中，同行评审过程通常超过一年，研究论文超过 100 页的情况很常见。）即使已知一个解是正确的，也许通过形式验证，外部观察者也可能难以区分重大进展与琐碎结果。

items.en: This evaluation gap has enabled a surge in hyperbolic or misleading messaging regarding AI-generated mathematical content. Instead of using academic publishing venues with vetting and review by professional mathematicians, such claims are often propagated through social media channels. While AI proponents have obvious incentives to exaggerate the mathematical capabilities of AI, a somewhat perverse incentive has also arisen for mathematicians to overstate the contribution of AI to their own work, because doing so leads to a significant increase in publicity and attention. Moreover, misinformation propagates easily in this domain because the audience is ill-equipped to evaluate the claims.
items.zh: 这种评估差距使得关于 AI 生成数学内容的夸张或误导性信息激增。此类声明通常通过社交媒体渠道传播，而非使用经过专业数学家审查的学术出版场所。虽然 AI 支持者有明显的动机夸大 AI 的数学能力，但数学家也出现了一种有点反常的激励，即夸大 AI 对自己工作的贡献，因为这样做会显著增加知名度和关注度。此外，由于受众缺乏评估此类声明的能力，错误信息在该领域容易传播。

items.en: Since we are developing tools for AI-assisted mathematics research, we also felt a responsibility to contribute to the discussion on responsible documentation, evaluation and communication of AI-generated results. In order to inform our thinking, we held a discussion with several mathematicians to understand the concerns of the mathematical community regarding AI.131313Mathematicians consulted (outside those among the authors of this paper) included Jarod Alper⋆, Kevin Barreto, Sourav Chatterjee, Otis Chodosh, Michael Hutchings, Daniel Litt, Ciprian Manolescu, Mona Merling, Johannes Schmitt, Ravi Vakil, Shengtong Zhang. ⋆Affiliated with Google DeepMind. One of the dominant themes of the discussion was the desire for transparency, both about the role of the AI and the technical importance of AI-assisted results. Motivated by this, we will define one possible framework for describing autonomous mathematics, and contextualize our own results within it for illustration. This should be considered as only a starting point in a broader discussion of responsible scientific communication. If any such standards are to be universally adopted, they must arise organically from the mathematical community.
items.zh: 由于我们正在开发用于 AI 辅助数学研究的工具，我们也感到有责任为关于 AI 生成结果的负责任记录、评估和传播的讨论做出贡献。为了启发我们的思考，我们与几位数学家进行了讨论，以了解数学界对 AI 的担忧。131313咨询的数学家（不包括本文作者）包括 Jarod Alper⋆、Kevin Barreto、Sourav Chatterjee、Otis Chodosh、Michael Hutchings、Daniel Litt、Ciprian Manolescu、Mona Merling、Johannes Schmitt、Ravi Vakil、Shengtong Zhang。⋆隶属于 Google DeepMind。讨论的一个主要主题是希望透明度，既关于 AI 的角色，也关于 AI 辅助结果的技术重要性。受此启发，我们将定义一个可能的框架来描述自主数学，并为了说明而将我们自己的结果置于其中进行背景分析。这应被视为更广泛的负责任科学传播讨论的起点。如果要普遍采用任何此类标准，它们必须有机地源自数学界。

items.en: Autonomous driving is classified according to standard “Levels”, measuring the degree of autonomy, defined by the Society of Automotive Engineers (SAE). A meaningful taxonomy of autonomous mathematics should have at least two dimensions, with one axis for the level of autonomy and another for the level of mathematical significance141414A similar example of such a division is in Epoch AI’s FrontierMath open problems, which are rated as “moderately interesting,” “solid result,” “major advance,” or “breakthrough.” epochai:2026:frontiermathopen. Maintaining a coarse division while preserving essential distinctions would minimize subjective disputes. We divide our own results into three broad levels of autonomy, described in Table 6.
items.zh: 自动驾驶根据美国汽车工程师学会（SAE）定义的标准“等级”进行分类，衡量自主程度。一个有意义的自主数学分类应至少有两个维度，一个轴用于自主程度，另一个轴用于数学重要性水平141414这种划分的一个类似例子是 Epoch AI 的 FrontierMath 开放问题，它们被评级为“中等有趣”、“扎实结果”、“重大进展”或“突破”。epochai:2026:frontiermathopen。保持粗略划分同时保留基本区别将最小化主观争议。我们将自己的结果分为三个广泛的自主程度，如表 6 所述。

items.en: In these terms, we consider the paper [Feng2026] to be of Level A, as the human contribution is only in expository aspects, aside from posing the original questions. The results in [erdos] are also of Level A insofar as the role of humans was only to correct minor inaccuracies. We consider the papers [LeeSeo2026] and [BKKKZ] to be of Level C: both humans and AI contributed essential aspects of the papers. We consider the papers [FYZ4] and [ACGKMP] to be of Level H: while the written papers have non-trivial contributions from AI, these contributions form a minor portion of the paper and the human authors could have supplied those parts themselves.
items.zh: 按照这些术语，我们认为论文 [Feng2026] 属于 A 级，因为除了提出原始问题外，人类贡献仅在于阐述方面。[erdos] 中的结果也属于 A 级，因为人类的作用仅限于纠正小的不准确之处。我们认为论文 [LeeSeo2026] 和 [BKKKZ] 属于 C 级：人类和 AI 都对论文的关键方面做出了贡献。我们认为论文 [FYZ4] 和 [ACGKMP] 属于 H 级：虽然书面论文中有 AI 的非平凡贡献，但这些贡献构成了论文的次要部分，人类作者本可以自己提供这些部分。

items.en: Even with these broad subdivisions, there can be some ambiguity. A possible situation is that interacting with AI leads human mathematicians to a key idea or reference, but the AI itself does not truly make its own mathematical contribution. In other words, the AI contributes only via “inspiration”. Although the research may not have happened without AI assistance, we would consider such instances as Level H.
items.zh: 即使有这些粗略的划分，仍可能存在一些模糊性。一种可能的情况是，与AI的互动引导人类数学家获得关键想法或参考文献，但AI本身并未真正做出自己的数学贡献。换言之，AI仅通过“启发”做出贡献。尽管没有AI协助，研究可能不会发生，但我们认为此类实例属于H级。

items.en: We emphasize at the outset that these Levels fail to capture the depth and diversity of human mathematics research, and are only meant to contextualize autonomous contributions. Given the inherent subjectivity of mathematical significance, and the proven difficulty of comparing the work of (human) mathematicians, we felt that the Levels of Mathematical Significance should be coarse enough that they do not differentiate human-generated research. A possible exception is that there is near-universal consensus on the top 5 journals in mathematics151515In alphabetical order, these are: Acta Math., Annals of Math., Inventiones Math., Journal of the AMS, Publ. Math. IHES. We expect that any proposed list of “top NN journals” for a different value of NN would be met with significant contention, as would any attempted internal ranking of these five., so it could make sense to anchor one level to them. One would expect to find major advances published in these journals; as far as we are aware, no autonomous result has yet come close to that standard. A final category could be reserved for truly landmark breakthroughs of once-in-a-generation stature.161616Examples might include the proof of Fermat’s Last Theorem wiles1995modular, taylor1995ring or Perelman’s proof of the Poincaré Conjecture perelman2002entropy, perelman2003ricci, perelman2003finite. Although evident, it seems worth emphasizing that the Level of Mathematical Significance can only be evaluated by mathematicians, who are moreover experts on the particular domain of the problem.
items.zh: 我们首先强调，这些层级未能捕捉人类数学研究的深度和多样性，仅旨在为自主贡献提供背景。鉴于数学意义固有的主观性，以及比较（人类）数学家工作的已知难度，我们认为数学意义层级应足够粗略，以至于不区分人类生成的研究。一个可能的例外是，数学界对前五大期刊151515按字母顺序排列，它们是：Acta Math.、Annals of Math.、Inventiones Math.、Journal of the AMS、Publ. Math. IHES。我们预计，任何针对不同NN值提出的“前NN大期刊”列表都会引发重大争议，同样，任何对这五种期刊的内部排名也会如此。因此，将某一层级锚定于这些期刊是合理的。人们期望在这些期刊上看到重大进展；据我们所知，目前尚无自主结果接近该标准。最后一个类别可保留给真正具有划时代意义的突破性成果。161616例子可能包括费马大定理的证明[wiles1995modular, taylor1995ring]或佩雷尔曼对庞加莱猜想的证明[perelman2002entropy, perelman2003ricci, perelman2003finite]。尽管显而易见，但值得强调的是，数学意义层级只能由数学家评估，而且他们必须是该问题特定领域的专家。

items.en: In these terms, we view our solutions to Erdős-652, 654, 935, 1040 as Level A0, and our solution to Erdős-1051 as Level A1. We view [Feng2026] as Level A2, [BKKKZ] and [LeeSeo2026] as Level C2, and [ACGKMP] and [FYZ4] as Level H2. We reiterate that the variation within Level 2 is deliberately wide to encompass the vast majority of papers written by professional mathematicians, ranging from very minor to extremely influential works. This appears to be the only way to avoid subjective disputes about human research. Because of the exceptional width of this category, the public should not understand a paper of level C2 or A2 as indicating that AI tools have reached the level of human mathematicians. For example, we view our own results within Level 2 [ACGKMP, BKKKZ, Feng2026, FYZ4, LeeSeo2026] as being very spread out in significance and difficulty.
items.zh: 据此，我们将对Erdős-652、654、935、1040的解答视为A0级，对Erdős-1051的解答视为A1级。我们将[Feng2026]视为A2级，[BKKKZ]和[LeeSeo2026]视为C2级，[ACGKMP]和[FYZ4]视为H2级。我们重申，第2级内的变化故意设置得很宽泛，以涵盖专业数学家撰写的绝大多数论文，从非常次要到极具影响力的作品。这似乎是避免关于人类研究的主观争议的唯一方法。由于该类别异常宽泛，公众不应将C2或A2级的论文理解为AI工具已达到人类数学家的水平。例如，我们认为我们在第2级内的成果[ACGKMP, BKKKZ, Feng2026, FYZ4, LeeSeo2026]在重要性和难度上分布非常分散。

items.en: Another theme of concern in the discussion with mathematicians was how best to document AI-assisted mathematics, both in the interest of helping the community achieve effective usage of AI tools, and also in regulating the promotion of AI involvement as an attention-getting mechanism. Several examples of AI-assisted mathematics papers have recently appeared in the literature [bryan2026motivic, jang2025point, schmitt2025extremal], with each featuring its own method of documenting the AI contributions. In our work, the interactions were clean enough to be neatly captured by a “Human-AI Interaction (HAI) Card”, which we display below for the papers [Feng2026, LeeSeo2026, BKKKZ]. This terminology is inspired by the “model card” concept in describing AI models.
items.zh: 与数学家讨论中关注的另一个主题是如何最好地记录AI辅助数学，既为了帮助社区有效使用AI工具，也为了规范将AI参与作为吸引注意力的机制。最近文献中出现了几个AI辅助数学论文的例子[bryan2026motivic, jang2025point, schmitt2025extremal]，每个都采用了各自记录AI贡献的方法。在我们的工作中，交互足够清晰，可以简洁地通过“人机交互（HAI）卡”来捕捉，我们在下面为论文[Feng2026, LeeSeo2026, BKKKZ]展示了这一点。该术语受描述AI模型时“模型卡”概念的启发。

items.en: In other instances, it may not be realistic to capture the interactions with the same template. While the specific presentation may be left to an author’s discretion, the field would benefit from a common baseline for transparency. For Level C or Level A results, where AI input is deemed essential, a possible baseline would be to expose at least the most important raw prompts and outputs that contain the essential new insights generated by AI. Specific standards are left for the community of mathematicians to decide. We note that, in a related direction, Tao has initiated an online discussion of “best practices for incorporating AI usage” tao2024bestpractices for mathematicians.
items.zh: 在其他情况下，用同一模板捕捉交互可能不现实。虽然具体呈现方式可由作者自行决定，但该领域将受益于一个共同的透明度基线。对于C级或A级结果（其中AI输入被视为必不可少），一个可能的基线是至少公开包含AI生成的关键新见解的最重要的原始提示和输出。具体标准留待数学界决定。我们注意到，在相关方向上，Tao已为数学家发起了一场关于“融入AI使用的最佳实践”的在线讨论[tao2024bestpractices]。

full.sec: 6 Reflections on the Impact of AI in Mathematics
full.secZh: 6 对AI在数学中影响的反思

items.en: To date, hype notwithstanding, the impact of artificial intelligence on pure mathematics research has been limited. While our results do solve some problems that seem to have eluded experts, they do not indicate that artificial intelligence has matched, or will match, the capabilities of human mathematicians. Rather, they illustrate how certain comparative advantages of AI models over humans can be useful for certain kinds of problems. This perhaps clarifies the directions where human researchers can expect the most impact from AI in the near future.
items.zh: 迄今为止，尽管存在炒作，人工智能对纯数学研究的影响仍然有限。虽然我们的结果确实解决了一些似乎难倒了专家的问题，但它们并不表明人工智能已经达到或将要达到人类数学家的能力。相反，它们说明了AI模型相对于人类的某些比较优势如何对某些类型的问题有用。这或许阐明了人类研究人员在不久的将来可以预期从AI中获得最大影响的领域。

items.en: A first observation is that AI models exhibit a form of intelligence that diverges significantly from that of human scientists. In any specific subject, frontier models have much shallower knowledge than a domain expert, but they also possess superhuman breadth of knowledge, which could be the key to unlocking certain problems. The simple fact that artificial intelligence differs from human intelligence presents the possibility that it is better suited for solving some types of problems, for example those requiring vast memory, computation, or breadth of knowledge.
items.zh: 第一个观察结果是，AI模型展现出一种与人类科学家显著不同的智能形式。在任何特定主题上，前沿模型的知识深度远不及领域专家，但它们拥有超人的知识广度，这可能是解决某些问题的关键。人工智能与人类智能不同这一简单事实表明，它可能更适合解决某些类型的问题，例如那些需要巨大记忆、算力或知识广度的问题。

items.en: Another comparative strength of AI is that it is not constrained by human physical limitations. It is likely that many open questions lie within the reach of existing techniques, but are not resolved because of limited time and attention from the right experts, as demonstrated by our results on the Erdős problems erdos. This reinforces the point that AI is bottlenecked by very different factors compared to humans, which can be an advantage in the right context.
items.zh: AI的另一个比较优势是不受人类生理限制。许多未解决问题可能通过现有技术即可解决，但由于缺乏合适专家的时间和关注而未被解决，正如我们在Erdős问题[erdos]上的结果所证明的那样。这强化了AI与人类相比受制于截然不同的因素，在适当背景下这可能是一个优势。

items.en: Recent advances in large language models have yielded systems capable of sophisticated mathematical reasoning deepmind:gemini2p5deepthink, openai:2025:gpt5_2, moving beyond standard benchmarks to competition-level problems. Notably, these models have achieved gold-medal standards at the International Mathematics Olympiad (IMO) imo-gold and demonstrated proficiency on difficult benchmarks like IMO-ProofBench luong2025robustmathematicalreasoning. New, more challenging benchmarks continue to emerge as the field shifts to focus on research-level math glazer2024frontiermath, nie2025uqassessinglanguagemodels, tao2026erdosai.
items.zh: 大型语言模型的最新进展已产生能够进行复杂数学推理的系统[deepmind:gemini2p5deepthink, openai:2025:gpt5_2]，超越了标准基准，达到竞赛级问题。值得注意的是，这些模型已在国际数学奥林匹克（IMO）中获得金牌标准[imo-gold]，并在IMO-ProofBench[luong2025robustmathematicalreasoning]等困难基准上展示了熟练程度。随着该领域转向关注研究级数学[glazer2024frontiermath, nie2025uqassessinglanguagemodels, tao2026erdosai]，新的更具挑战性的基准不断涌现。

items.en: Already, AI has been successfully applied to assist mathematical research. Examples include research on extremal descendant integrals on moduli spaces [schmitt2025extremal], motivic classes of genus zero maps [bryan2026motivic], and convergence rates for Nesterov’s method [jang2025point]. Beyond these specific cases, AI has been used in other works across discrete analysis, convex analysis, and combinatorics [ivanisvili2025counterexamplemajorityoptimalitynicd, salim2025acceleratingmathematicalresearchlanguage]. Other studies show AI is useful for solving accessible conjectures or assisting with problems that require mathematical exploration [feldman2025godeltestlargelanguage, georgiev2025mathematicalexplorationdiscoveryscale]. Even more broadly, woodruff2026acceleratingscientificresearchgemini demonstrates a multitude of techniques and case studies for AI-assisted research across several mathematics-adjacent domains, including theoretical computer science, economics, and physics.
items.zh: AI已被成功应用于辅助数学研究。例子包括模空间上极值后代积分的研究[schmitt2025extremal]、亏格零映射的动机类[bryan2026motivic]以及Nesterov方法的收敛速度[jang2025point]。除了这些具体案例，AI还被用于离散分析、凸分析和组合学等其他工作[ivanisvili2025counterexamplemajorityoptimalitynicd, salim2025acceleratingmathematicalresearchlanguage]。其他研究表明，AI有助于解决可访问的猜想或辅助需要数学探索的问题[feldman2025godeltestlargelanguage, georgiev2025mathematicalexplorationdiscoveryscale]。更广泛地，woodruff2026acceleratingscientificresearchgemini展示了跨多个数学相关领域（包括理论计算机科学、经济学和物理学）的AI辅助研究的多种技术和案例研究。

items.en: A variety of human-AI collaborative implementations have emerged, ranging from fully agentic architectures with self-verification and refinement huang2025winninggoldimo2025, bryan2026motivic, to exploration methods such as AlphaEvolve novikov2025alphaevolvecodingagentscientific, wang2025thetaevolvetesttimelearningopen, yuksekgonul2026learningdiscovertesttime, to more collaboration-focused methods such as interactive protocols suggesting how to integrate AI as a research assistant li2025advancingmathematicalresearchhumanai, henkel2025mathematiciansassistantintegratingai. Despite the surge in attention bubeck2025earlyscienceaccelerationexperiments, zheng2025automationautonomysurveylarge, the community is still in the early stages of adapting AI for mathematical research, making it essential to establish transparent standards for how AI-generated mathematics is documented, evaluated, and communicated.
items.zh: 多种人机协作实现方式已经出现，从具有自我验证和优化的完全智能体式架构[huang2025winninggoldimo2025, bryan2026motivic]，到AlphaEvolve[novikov2025alphaevolvecodingagentscientific, wang2025thetaevolvetesttimelearningopen, yuksekgonul2026learningdiscovertesttime]等探索方法，再到更注重协作的方法，如建议如何将AI整合为研究助手的交互协议[li2025advancingmathematicalresearchhumanai, henkel2025mathematiciansassistantintegratingai]。尽管关注度激增[bubeck2025earlyscienceaccelerationexperiments, zheng2025automationautonomysurveylarge]，社区仍处于将AI适应于数学研究的早期阶段，因此建立透明标准以记录、评估和传播AI生成的数学至关重要。

items.en: Ultimately, we believe that AI will become a tool that enhances rather than replaces mathematicians. Currently, natural language models struggle to reason reliably without human intervention to correct mistakes and hallucinations, while formal verification systems are not yet capable of even formulating the questions of interest on most research frontiers. For this reason, we have introduced specialized math reasoning agents, incorporating informal natural language verification, to help mathematicians harness the benefits of AI.
items.zh: 最终，我们相信AI将成为增强而非取代数学家的工具。目前，自然语言模型在没有人类干预纠正错误和幻觉的情况下难以可靠推理，而形式化验证系统甚至无法在大多数研究前沿表述感兴趣的问题。因此，我们引入了专门的数学推理智能体，结合非正式自然语言验证，以帮助数学家利用AI的优势。

items.en: Special thanks to the following experts for mathematical discussions on the work: Jarod Alper, Kevin Barreto, Thomas Bloom, Sourav Chatterjee, Otis Chodosh, Michael Hutchings, Seongbin Jeon, Youngbeom Jin, Aiden Yuchan Jung, Jiwon Kang, Jimin Kim, Vjekoslav Kovač, Daniel Litt, Ciprian Manolescu, Mona Merling, Agustin Moreno, Carl Schildkraut, Johannes Schmitt, Insuk Seo, Jaehyeon Seo, Cheng-Chiang Tsai, Ravi Vakil, Zhiwei Yun, Shengtong Zhang, Wei Zhang, Yufei Zhao. We also would like to thank Adam Brown, Alex Davies, Thomas Hubert, Pushmeet Kohli, Vinay Ramasesh, Eugénie Rives, Benoit Schillings, Divy Thakkar for feedback and support of the work.
items.zh: 特别感谢以下专家在数学讨论中的贡献：Jarod Alper, Kevin Barreto, Thomas Bloom, Sourav Chatterjee, Otis Chodosh, Michael Hutchings, Seongbin Jeon, Youngbeom Jin, Aiden Yuchan Jung, Jiwon Kang, Jimin Kim, Vjekoslav Kovač, Daniel Litt, Ciprian Manolescu, Mona Merling, Agustin Moreno, Carl Schildkraut, Johannes Schmitt, Insuk Seo, Jaehyeon Seo, Cheng-Chiang Tsai, Ravi Vakil, Zhiwei Yun, Shengtong Zhang, Wei Zhang, Yufei Zhao。我们还要感谢Adam Brown, Alex Davies, Thomas Hubert, Pushmeet Kohli, Vinay Ramasesh, Eugénie Rives, Benoit Schillings, Divy Thakkar对工作的反馈和支持。
